children.heading: Authority to award franchises; public rights-of-way and easements; equal access to service; time for provision of service; assurances
children.label: a Authority to award franchises; public rights-of-way and easements; equal access to service; time for provision of service; assurances

children.text: A franchising authority may award, in accordance with the provisions of this subchapter, 1 or more franchises within its jurisdiction; except that a franchising authority may not grant an exclusive franchise and may not unreasonably refuse to award an additional competitive franchise. Any applicant whose application for a second franchise has been denied by a final decision of the franchising authority may appeal such final decision pursuant to the provisions of for failure to comply with this subsection.section 555 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: that the safety, functioning, and appearance of the property and the convenience and safety of other persons not be adversely affected by the installation or construction of facilities necessary for a cable system;

children.label: B
children.text: that the cost of the installation, construction, operation, or removal of such facilities be borne by the cable operator or subscriber, or a combination of both; and

children.label: C
children.text: that the owner of the property be justly compensated by the cable operator for any damages caused by the installation, construction, operation, or removal of such facilities by the cable operator.

In awarding a franchise or franchises, a franchising authority shall assure that access to cable service is not denied to any group of potential residential cable subscribers because of the income of the residents of the local area in which such group resides.

children.label: A
children.text: shall allow the applicant’s cable system a reasonable period of time to become capable of providing cable service to all households in the franchise area;

children.label: B
children.text: may require adequate assurance that the cable operator will provide adequate public, educational, and governmental access channel capacity, facilities, or financial support; and

children.label: C
children.text: may require adequate assurance that the cable operator has the financial, technical, or legal qualifications to provide cable service.

children.heading: No cable service without franchise; exception under prior law
children.label: b No cable service without franchise; exception under prior law

children.text: Except to the extent provided in paragraph (2) and subsection (f), a cable operator may not provide cable service without a franchise.
Paragraph (1) shall not require any person lawfully providing cable service without a franchise on , to obtain a franchise unless the franchising authority so requires.July 1, 1984

children.label: i
children.text: such cable operator or affiliate shall not be required to obtain a franchise under this subchapter for the provision of telecommunications services; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the provisions of this subchapter shall not apply to such cable operator or affiliate for the provision of telecommunications services.

children.label: B
children.text: A franchising authority may not impose any requirement under this subchapter that has the purpose or effect of prohibiting, limiting, restricting, or conditioning the provision of a telecommunications service by a cable operator or an affiliate thereof.

children.label: i
children.text: to discontinue the provision of a telecommunications service, or

children.label: ii
children.text: to discontinue the operation of a cable system, to the extent such cable system is used for the provision of a telecommunications service, by reason of the failure of such cable operator or affiliate thereof to obtain a franchise or franchise renewal under this subchapter with respect to the provision of such telecommunications service.

children.label: D
children.text: Except as otherwise permitted by sections 531 and 532 of this title, a franchising authority may not require a cable operator to provide any telecommunications service or facilities, other than institutional networks, as a condition of the initial grant of a franchise, a franchise renewal, or a transfer of a franchise.

children.heading: Status of cable system as common carrier or utility
children.label: c Status of cable system as common carrier or utility
children.text: Any cable system shall not be subject to regulation as a common carrier or utility by reason of providing any cable service.

children.heading: Informational tariffs; regulation by States; “State” defined
children.label: d Informational tariffs; regulation by States; “State” defined

children.text: A State or the Commission may require the filing of informational tariffs for any intrastate communications service provided by a cable system, other than cable service, that would be subject to regulation by the Commission or any State if offered by a common carrier subject, in whole or in part, to subchapter II of this chapter. Such informational tariffs shall specify the rates, terms, and conditions for the provision of such service, including whether it is made available to all subscribers generally, and shall take effect on the date specified therein.

Nothing in this subchapter shall be construed to affect the authority of any State to regulate any cable operator to the extent that such operator provides any communication service other than cable service, whether offered on a common carrier or private contract basis.
For purposes of this subsection, the term “State” has the meaning given it in .section 153 of this title

children.heading: State regulation of facilities serving subscribers in multiple dwelling units
children.label: e State regulation of facilities serving subscribers in multiple dwelling units
children.text: Nothing in this subchapter shall be construed to affect the authority of any State to license or otherwise regulate any facility or combination of facilities which serves only subscribers in one or more multiple unit dwellings under common ownership, control, or management and which does not use any public right-of-way.

children.heading: Local or municipal authority as multichannel video programming distributor
children.label: f Local or municipal authority as multichannel video programming distributor

children.text: prohibit a local or municipal authority that is also, or is affiliated with, a franchising authority from operating as a multichannel video programming distributor in the franchise area, notwithstanding the granting of one or more franchises by such franchising authority; or
require such local or municipal authority to secure a franchise to operate as a multichannel video programming distributor.
